guardrails.title: Single Responsibility
guardrails.body: Each spec shall address one concern. Do not bundle unrelated changes into a single specification.

guardrails.title: Unambiguous Requirements
guardrails.body: Requirements shall avoid vague adjectives (e.g. "appropriate", "fast", "easy") and instead state verifiable conditions.

guardrails.title: Complete Context
guardrails.body: Each requirement shall pair a trigger condition (When / If) with an expected behavior (shall). Requirements without context or expected outcome are incomplete.

guardrails.title: No Hardcoded Secrets
guardrails.body: Source code shall not contain API keys, passwords, tokens, or other secrets directly. Externalize them via environment variables or configuration files.

guardrails.title: No Silent Error Swallowing
guardrails.body: Empty catch blocks or code that silently discards errors are prohibited. Errors shall be logged or re-thrown.

guardrails.title: Prioritize Requirements
guardrails.body: When requirements exceed three items, specify their priority order. This prevents scope creep and ensures the most critical items are addressed first.

guardrails.title: Impact on Existing Features
guardrails.body: Changes shall list their impact on existing features. If no existing features are affected, state so explicitly.
